mnemonics: IF-THEN Rules are the BRICKS of a Knowledge Base: Building Reasoning In Controlled Knowledge Systems.
keyPoints: Rules use an IF-THEN structure to represent conditional knowledge.
A collection of rules forms a rule-based system, which can use forward or backward chaining for inference.
Rules are modular, easy to understand, and good for representing deterministic knowledge from human experts.
commonMistakes: Creating rules with overly broad or overlapping conditions, leading to conflicts and unintended inferences.
Attempting to use rules for problems that require representing uncertainty or probabilistic relationships, which they handle poorly.
